title: Shattering Turkish Sage Kunefe
description: A spicy and cozy Turkish dish featuring sage and smoke, topped with creamy cheese and a crispy dessert-like shell. Perfect for a comforting snack or dessert.
instructions: Thaw the phyllo dough according to package instructions.
In a small bowl, mix together smoked paprika, ground cumin, and cayenne pepper.
Brush the edges of the phyllo sheets with unsalted butter.
Place a sheet of phyllo dough on a flat surface and brush with butter.
Sprinkle a quarter of the sage leaves on the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the dough in half to form a triangle and press gently to seal.
Repeat with the remaining dough sheets and sage leaves.
In a separate bowl, mix together ricotta cheese, shredded cheddar cheese, honey, and chopped walnuts.
Pour the cheese mixture into the center of the dough triangles and spread evenly.
Place the triangles on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 20-25 minutes or until golden brown.
Drizzle the rum or apple cider vinegar over the kunefe and serve immediately.
